subject: Top 5 border falsehoods pushed by Congressional Democrats and the media
body:
Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen testified before Congress yesterday about the National Emergency on our southern border. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c448fa1cc5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 7, 2019
---
| | |
# **Top 5 border falsehoods pushed by Congressional Democrats and the media**

**Homeland Security Secretary** Kirstjen Nielsen testified before Congress yesterday about the National Emergency on our southern border. In reporting the latest statistics from U.S. border officials, she confronted the Democrats’ no. 1 immigration myth head-on. “We face a crisis—a real, serious, and sustained crisis at our borders,” she said.

**The reason Democrats** defend the status quo is not because they or anyone else believe that America’s broken immigration system is working. Indeed, the problem has only gotten worse since former President Obama himself called it a “humanitarian crisis” nearly five years ago.

**But acknowledging such a crisis exists** today would make Democrats accountable for working with President Trump to help solve it. Their leaders have already gone on the record to say they will not. Instead, they hope to keep a lid on the ugly, painful truths behind the emergency unfolding along our southern border.

**It is time** to set the record straight:
1. **Myth:** There is no crisis at the border.

**Fact** : More than 76,000 people attempted to cross our border illegally last month alone—the most of any February during the past 12 years.

2. **Myth:** The problem at the border used to be much worse.

**Fact:** Today, we face an unprecedented number of illegal immigrants that loopholes prevent from being returned quickly to their home countries. Criminal smugglers exploit these rulings and use children as leverage to gain entry.

3. **Myth:** A wall wouldn’t help stop the flow of drugs.

**Fact:** Not only do we not know the amount of drugs smuggled between ports of entry, but building the wall will help law enforcement personnel carry out their missions more safely and effectively in remote areas of the border.

[Top 5 Border Falsehoods Pushed by Congressional Democrats and the Media](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ece7c8558a&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch:****[The President has a duty to fix this crisis, Press Secretary Sanders says](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ded60e92e2&e=3ee4985eea)**
---
|
---
|
## **30 years ago, the Czech Republic fought off Communism**

**This year,** America and the Czech Republic celebrate the 30-year anniversary of the inspiring Velvet Revolution, when the people of Czechoslovakia took back their freedom and ended decades of Communist oppression.

**President Trump and First Lady** **Melania Trump** welcomed Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis and Mrs. Monika Babisova to the White House today. Upon their arrival, the President congratulated Prime Minister Babis on this historic anniversary. He also noted that this year marks the 20th anniversary of the Czech Republic joining NATO.

**“****We share the view** that peace, security, and prosperity depend on the cooperation of sovereign nations,” President Trump and the Prime Minister said in a joint statement today. “Strength at home is a necessary foundation for peace and security abroad.”

[Read the full statement from President Trump and Prime Minister Babis.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4a661e6e8a&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Join me for a major announcement in South Florida
body:
On March 20, I'll be announcing...

Friend -
We came so close to winning our state back last year. It still stings -- especially when I think about the people who lose when we lose elections.

But what I've realized, is that the legacy of our campaign was never going to be defined by victory or defeat on one day.

**Last year, we inspired the biggest progressive movement in the history of our state. **Over 70,000 of you took Florida's future into your own hands, volunteering to knock on doors, make phone calls, or text your friends.

And on Election Day, more than four million Floridians, from every corner of the state -- black, white, Latino, rich, poor, men, women, young and old -- showed up to support our campaign, because we believed in hope, decency, and a government that represents all of us.

**And let me tell you: I still believe.**

[**_That 's why I'm inviting you to join me on March 20 in South Florida when I announce the future of this movement. Will you join me? _**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c6/9e00690/685e3c41/2400a839/2628786569/VEsH/ "http://bringithomefl.org")

We'll announce the details soon, so save the date -- I need you there.

Because what we fought for last year still holds true today. Health care should be a right and not a privilege. Teachers should be paid what they are worth. Our water and air should be safe for our children.

**And most importantly: we need to do everything in our power to make Donald Trump a one-term president.**

This fight is about the future of our state and our nation. I'm not going anywhere -- and I know neither are you. We have to stand strong and speak out.

[**_I believe that we will win. I 'll see you on March 20._**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c6/9e00690/685e3c41/2400a839/2628786569/VEsE/ "http://bringithomefl.org")

Let's bring it home,

Andrew Gillum

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by Forward Florida, P.O. Box 7453 Tallahassee, FL 32314. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c6/9e00690/685e3c41/2400a838/2628786569/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb35c6/9e00690/685e3c41/2400a83b/2628786569/VEsC/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/").

The latest "election reform" bill is nothing but bad news.

My colleagues across the aisle want you to think this will change the whole political game, but the truth is that _**this is nothing but a political power grab designed to push an ultra-left political agenda.**_

This crooked legislation doesn't "reform" anything, it mandates a huge federal takeover of elections. It takes away state’s rights to regulate voter ID, empowers corporate interests, and sticks the American taxpayer with the bill for every politician’s expensive campaign.

If H.R. 1 passes, Congressional candidates could potentially receive $4.3 MILLION of taxpayer dollars to fund their campaigns! _Now can you see why so many liberals are lining up to support the bill?_

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: International Women’s Day! 🎉
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter, you know that when we support, encourage and empower women, good things happen for everyone.
| | Happy International Women's Day, Peter!

Every year on March 8, we dedicate the day to honoring the contributions, courage and sacrifices of countless women across the world.

It is because of their sacrifices and their willingness to upend conventional wisdom that I am here today, serving as the representative from Virginia's 7th District alongside a record 101 women in the U.S. House of Representatives.

But it is important to remember that we come together to not only celebrate the triumphs of the past and the accomplishments of the present, but to also remind ourselves of the inequalities that continue to exist that we are called to confront if we hope to secure an even brighter future.

The path forward won't be an easy one; we are called to honor the legacy of the trailblazers who came before us, who shattered glass ceilings and broke down barriers, by supporting the women in our communities right here and now. And from ensuring affordable healthcare to strengthening economic opportunity, the women of the 116th Congress are ready, willing and able to advocate for a more equitable, more just society.

After all, that is what today is all about.

[**Peter, you know that when we support, encourage and empower women, good things happen for everyone. That is why I hope you 'll consider lending your support to our re-election campaign as we work together to make the longstanding promise of International Women's Day a reality. >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/iECiNl6hjEm_oDF7gOmZy-0HGHGoc7yQQjGpfU1l4w4=/hBejwN62u7ITWWxDNhb-6B84OTcsCm_X01wVRKwLUtQ=?refcode=em_fr_2019.03.08_b1_as_all&amount=25)

All the best,

_-Abigail Spanberger_

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Who changes the world
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020 Exploratory Committee
body:
To all the brave, strong, fiery women:
Peter,
For so many women and especially young girls, there's a whole world of voices telling us to doubt ourselves. To be quiet. To wait our turn. But seeing brave, strong, fiery women batting that away and setting their own course can change everything. That was my grandmother for me.
My grandmother was my hero. She was a firebrand--larger than life in every way, from her personality to her wide vocabulary of swear words. She was an outspoken advocate in a time when women weren't part of the conversation, and I learned from her that my voice matters--that my advocacy and grassroots activism can make a difference.
**More girls and women deserve to see that there's nothing we can't do, and nowhere we don't belong.**
That's why this International Women's Day, I promise you that I will continue fearlessly fighting for all the issues we care about most, demanding to be heard, and I'm asking you to do the same. **[If you believe that women deserve equal pay for equal work; that women should have the right to safe reproductive health care; and that women's voices need to be heard and represented in every space at every level, add your name right now and commit to fighting for it.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-IWD?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2812-su-gfp-nd")**
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://act.kirstengillibrand.com/kg-IWD?firstName=Peter&lastName=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk2812-su-gfp-nd")
Together, I know we can blaze a better, more equitable and just path forward.
All my best,
Kirsten

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: A Hot US Job Market is Coaxing People in from the Sidelines
body:
“A surprisingly strong burst of job growth over the past year has led many economists to wonder: Where are all the workers coming from?” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a15fe0891a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **A Hot US Job Market is Coaxing People in from the Sidelines**

“A surprisingly strong burst of job growth over the past year has led many economists to wonder: Where are all the workers coming from?” Christopher Rugaber writes in _The Associated Press_.

“The pace of hiring in 2018 was the most robust in three years, and for a surprising reason: Many more people have decided to look for work than experts had expected. The influx of those job seekers, if sustained, could help extend an economic expansion that is already the second-longest on record.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1dfa593416&e=3ee4985eea)

**Breaking this morning** : [Wage growth is the fastest since 2009](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=519d69f247&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| In _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=90f318ba50&e=3ee4985eea)_ , James Freeman writes that the American jobs machine is roaring as “U.S. small businesses in February went on an historic hiring binge.” According to the latest employment report from the National Federation of Independent Business, small business job creation broke a 45-year record last month.
---
|
---
| “President Donald Trump’s 2020 budget proposal will include $100 million for a global women’s fund spearheaded by his daughter Ivanka Trump,” Catherine Lucey reports for _[The Associated Press](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=03d2e6b8ba&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The White House said the budget, expected to be released Monday, will include the funding for the Women’s Global Development and Prosperity Initiative. The administration last month launched the government-wide project, which she leads.”
---
|
---
| “One hundred eleven miles of new or replacement wall is either being built or is in progress on the southern border after Trump’s first two years in office,” Saagar Enjeti reports in [_The Daily Caller_.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a3ea0abfb9&e=3ee4985eea) “All told, the administration has secured funding for approximately 445 miles of the total 722 miles desired by the Trump administration, a Caller analysis finds.”
---
|
---
| First Lady Melania Trump “made her third official visit as first lady to the State Department on Thursday, passing out the International Women of Courage Awards,” Kate Bennett reports for [CNN](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7221c1802b&e=3ee4985eea). “Courage is what divides those who only talk about change from those who actually act to change. Courage takes sacrifice, bravery and humility -- it is the ability to put others first,” the First Lady said.
---
|
---
| In the _[Miami Herald](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cbf0c71f1a&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Hudson Institute Senior Fellow Nadia Schadlow writes that the Trump Administration’s W-GDP initiative will empower women across the globe. “As we observe International Women’s Day, Americans should make a bipartisan commitment to supporting W-GDP, which represents an enormous opportunity to spur growth and create stability by empowering women to use their untapped entrepreneurial talent and invest in their families, communities, and their countries.”

---
|

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: President Trump sees what’s happening in Indiana
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We’re taking Indiana’s success to the nation. Governor Holcomb has been appointed to President Donald J. Trump’s American Workforce Policy Advisory | | |
---|---|---
|
---
Friend,
**This is how we’re taking the Hoosier way to the entire nation.**
On Wednesday, Governor Holcomb attended the first-ever meeting of the American Workforce Policy Advisory Board at the White House – as one of only two sitting governors on this panel.
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=80eaf75e11734d5fb16dfb60b96a7d8a&_e=jaJv3oLYugutKDdABiYbBQk_u3KqXDEBOPIYzIIcScfIO0fJKgyvcfDjserWQzfyW2opOntD8aYTUIzPRi1hH90cQ4jGUcZEiZMkSc71SFJ-dQlX00T1H5_YDEJyALrV4COAUm7LUO0896oIla1UMefqSKj6ObvzCLk1oEkWDNzwWobN5GvdQb8qdxamybA9qIqeAt3yk1S7bN_cF6jWqM2a1dom7kJVYT6ODzHPUlapJl8SQ4C7ByI0x1fg4UFFXoPudqIExub0uHe9nuZ9ubXs6NY84Lz20BzcfbKnyulSI3_EdxXJK3__i0jit4LNfH0azpUwJlI6RH7wiqJD-VxQGDozslmIryO8GgZ65dI-gbR8z0q_wPhzKEEb7TG2ZLePsuU-NhOxy31DeowG8HlBOUvwy9c4MDPWLl5Vei0%3D)
[ **Watch video of Governor Holcomb thanking President Trump!** ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=80eaf75e11734d5fb16dfb60b96a7d8a&_e=jaJv3oLYugutKDdABiYbBQk_u3KqXDEBOPIYzIIcScfIO0fJKgyvcfDjserWQzfyW2opOntD8aYTUIzPRi1hH90cQ4jGUcZEiZMkSc71SFJ-dQlX00T1H5_YDEJyALrV4COAUm7LUO0896oIla1UMefqSKj6ObvzCLk1oEkWDNzwWobN5GvdQb8qdxamybA9qIqeAt3yk1S7bN_cF6jWqKrmM6LpacWe0ocT-UEUOSoPLIgCp7LN_YlJN1GjAVBKiQXGFLuv0mke50PRZcXN9gZL6pbclxhPLVmsTJky_wz62lk0vaZ8CWSDJC2IAASptkqjcWHj9J7pjhF-2_6b1S38rH3WpnKPxE0p36ZZGsd4VKNzJ1VSFvFrC1V9sTlwfExpm1KkyZftEQV7symZ8eVZOuMaDx8tXPSaztSx73U%3D)
With President Trump in attendance, Governor Holcomb highlighted how today more Hoosiers are skilling-up to fill high-wage, high-demand jobs -- and how **our partners in the Trump Administration are lettings states like Indiana be the ones to lead the way.**
It’s no accident that Governor Holcomb was chosen for this distinguished panel of workforce development experts and leaders. Indiana has been a model for the nation on this issue, and **the Trump Administration is eager to replicate our success in other states.**
Numbers released this week show that here in Indiana:
* The number of Hoosiers with education and training credentials beyond a high school diploma has been increasing over the last year!

* Since 2008, that number has **increased by 10%!**
* More and more Hoosiers are seeking workforce certificates -- not just traditional college degrees.

**The bottom line is that Governor Holcomb’s Next Level Jobs Program is working – Hoosiers see it, President Trump sees it, and soon enough, the entire nation will see it.** More than 5,000 Hoosiers have already completed a certificate program through Next Level Jobs... and that number keeps growing.
Our success is impossible to ignore, but ultimately Governor Holcomb’s work is just getting started. That’s because he is laser-focused on continuing to offer ALL Hoosiers the tools they need to grow their careers right here in Indiana.
**It’s a proud day when Indiana gets to help the rest of America find success through opportunity. We’ll continue to keep you up-to-date on Governor Holcomb’s important work on this task force, as he takes the Indiana model and Indiana success story to the nation!**
Thank you,

Holcomb Crew

name: Tina Smith
subject: Will you take 3 minutes to hear about the women I admire?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
Please read.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-march?refcode=rtw_20190308_em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Sturgeon Lake&state=MN&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto") Friend,

Today, on International Women's Day, I wanted to take some time to share with you women in my life who have inspired and supported me. I hope you can do the same and, if you can, send them a note of thanks.

My grandmother Avis Iden Mason is one of the women I think about. She was born in 1898, went to college (which was a big deal for women in those days), and then came home to Indiana to be the President of the community bank that her father founded. She did this while raising 7 children including my mother, and lived into her late 80s.

Dr. Josie Johnson is a friend, mentor and the most gracious, savvy, wise woman I know. She led a delegation from Minnesota to Mississippi in 1964 to witness the civil rights struggle and helped found the Minneapolis Department of Civil Rights. Later, Josie became the first African-American appointed to the Board of Regents at the University of Minnesota. I wish I could name every accomplishment and every life she has touched, but it's a tribute to her that the list is too long to name.

Thank you again for taking the time to read this message and I hope you have a wonderful International Women's Day!

Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. subject: President Trump & Governor Holcomb
body:
This week, Governor Holcomb went to Washington. And he didn’t just go...he went straight to the White House for the first American Workforce Policy...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/president-trump-governor-holcomb?e=1acd9bb3d6)
http://indiana.gop/2019springdinner?mc_cid=a6545d4654&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

President Trump & Governor Holcomb

This week, Governor Holcomb went to Washington. And he didn’t just go...he went straight to the White House for the first American Workforce Policy Advisory Board meeting to share directly with President Donald Trump some great news: Under the Trump Administration, today more Americans in states across the country – particularly in Indiana – are skilling up to fill the 21^st jobs of today and tomorrow.

Governor Holcomb said it best to the president during the meeting. “As you continue to trust us – the states – to own it and to prove it, we will show America what works and what doesn’t. And that will take us to the Next Level.”

That’s because President Trump understands that real solutions come from our states. And Governor Holcomb is leading the charge to deliver here in Indiana, from workforce development and beyond.

-Chairman Kyle Hupfer
GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS
https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=550325062155006&mc_cid=a6545d4654&mc_eid=[UNIQID]

Governor Holcomb is Helping Lead Indiana’s Workforce Growth

This week, Governor Eric Holcomb was at the White House with President Donald Trump for the first meeting of the American Workforce Policy Advisory Board. Check out the video here (https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=550325062155006&mc_cid=a6545d4654&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) !

He’s taking Indiana’s success story of skilling-up Hoosiers to fill high-wage, in-demand jobs to the nation! And this week, some great news broke regarding Indiana’s progress toward helping more Hoosiers skill-up:
* The number of Hoosiers with education and training credentials beyond a high school diploma has been increasing over the last year
* Since 2008, that number has increased by 10%
* More and more Hoosiers are seeking workforce certificates -- not just traditional college degrees

Tom O'Halleran is running for Congress in Arizona's 1st district, a critical race to getting Congress back on track. Click here to donate to Tom's campaign and help him put Arizona families first.
[](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d632388/1a8421e1/1981f6e46/5acbf6fd/2540318100/VEsH/ "https://action.tomohalleran.com/page/s/equalpay")

Hi Friend, Today is International Women's Day. And even after all of the progress we've made, the cards are still stacked against women across the country. There is no debate that two people doing the exact same job shouldn't be earning different amounts because of their gender. **This is unacceptable. And it has to change.** [**Sign this petition if you too believe men and women should earn equal pay for equal work. We only have 24 hours to collect 500 signatures.
